summary: Matched the 156-byte library function `lb_8000F9F8` in `src/melee/lb/lb_00F9.c`. The implementation operates on an `HSD_JObj*`, calls `lb_80011C18` on the child JObj with a newly named `JOBJ_UNK_B26` flag when a child exists, and calls the static DObj helper `lb_80011B74` on the JObj DObj when the JObj is not `JOBJ_PTCL` or `JOBJ_SPLINE`. The PR also tightened `lb_00F9.h` prototypes with typed arguments and address comments, added the missing JObj bit-26 macro in `jobj.h`, and updated scratch/config metadata. No review discussion was present in the slice.

key_files.reason: Added the matched C implementation of `lb_8000F9F8` plus a local inline flag helper and static declaration for `lb_80011B74`.
Replaced loose/void prototypes with address-commented, typed declarations including `void lb_8000F9F8(HSD_JObj* jobj)` and `void lb_80011C18(HSD_JObj* jobj, u32 flags)`.
Added `#define JOBJ_UNK_B26 (1 << 26)`, used by the new match.
Large assembly-label/comment churn from local/zero-based labels to absolute-address labels; the slice does not show this as direct logic change.
Scratch metadata was reordered/updated as part of integrating the match.

what_changed: Implemented `lb_8000F9F8(HSD_JObj* jobj)` in C, matching the PR-reported 156-byte function at 100%.
Added `static inline bool checkJObjFlags(HSD_JObj* jobj)` returning false when `jobj->flags` contains `JOBJ_PTCL` or `JOBJ_SPLINE`.
Added a static forward declaration `/* 011B74 */ static void lb_80011B74(HSD_DObj* dobj, u32 flags);` for the local DObj helper called by the matched function.
The function checks `HSD_JObjGetChild(jobj) != NULL` and, if present, calls `lb_80011C18(HSD_JObjGetChild(jobj), JOBJ_UNK_B26)`.
The function checks `checkJObjFlags(jobj) && HSD_JObjGetDObj(jobj) != NULL` before calling `lb_80011B74(HSD_JObjGetDObj(jobj), JOBJ_UNK_B26)`.
Added the JObj flag macro `JOBJ_UNK_B26` as bit 26 in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.h`.

Cleaned up `lb_00F9.h` by sorting/annotating declarations with address comments and replacing several `void`/`unk_t`/`s32`/`f32` spellings with project-style `UNK_T`, `int`, `float`, and concrete pointer types where evidenced.
Updated `config/GALE01/scratches.txt`; the excerpt mostly shows reordered scratch entries rather than a semantic source change.
smart_moves: Factored the JObj flag exclusion into a tiny `static inline` helper, making the bit-test readable while still allowing the compiler to emit the needed branch shape for a small match.
Introduced a named `JOBJ_UNK_B26` macro instead of using raw `(1 << 26)` at call sites, making future JObj flag work easier to search and audit.
Typed the public prototype for `lb_8000F9F8` as `HSD_JObj*`, which documents the function’s real input and enables natural use of `HSD_JObjGetChild`, `HSD_JObjGetDObj`, and `jobj->flags`.
Preserved address comments in the header (`/* 00F9F8 */`, `/* 011C18 */`, etc.), which helps future decomp work map declarations back to symbol addresses.
Used existing baselib accessors/macros (`HSD_JObjGetChild`, `HSD_JObjGetDObj`, `JOBJ_PTCL`, `JOBJ_SPLINE`) rather than open-coding struct offsets except for the direct flag field test.
decomp_lessons: For small JObj flag functions, a readable inline boolean helper can be enough to control codegen without resorting to opaque gotos or assembly.
When a match reveals a previously unknown flag bit, add it to the shared baselib header immediately so later matches reuse the same macro and avoid duplicate raw constants.

Do not leave newly matched functions as `void` prototypes in headers; even minimal concrete types like `HSD_JObj*` and `u32 flags` improve both compile checking and future reverse-engineering context.
Address-sorted prototype blocks with address comments are useful in partially decompiled files because they preserve map order while allowing functions to be matched incrementally.

Repeated accessor calls in source, such as calling `HSD_JObjGetChild(jobj)` in both the condition and argument, may be intentional for matching; avoid prematurely caching values unless the generated code still matches.
naming_conventions: `JOBJ_UNK_B26` follows existing JObj flag macro style: `JOBJ_` prefix plus descriptive/unknown suffix and bit index marker.
Matched functions in `lb_00F9.h` are documented with leading address comments, e.g. `/* 00F9F8 */ void lb_8000F9F8(HSD_JObj* jobj);`.

Local helper naming used camelCase (`checkJObjFlags`) rather than address-only naming because it is a source-only inline helper, while real symbols retain address names such as `lb_8000F9F8` and `lb_80011C18`.
Unknown project types are normalized to `UNK_T` in the touched header rather than the older lowercase `unk_t` spelling.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The matched source used a ternary-style flag helper: `return jobj->flags & (JOBJ_PTCL | JOBJ_SPLINE) ? false : true;`, which likely helped express the exact boolean polarity needed by the compiler.
The function structure is two independent `if` blocks, not an `else`; child handling and DObj handling both run when their conditions allow.
The DObj path combines the flag check and null DObj check with `&&`, preserving short-circuit behavior before calling `lb_80011B74`.

The source repeats `HSD_JObjGetChild(jobj)` and `HSD_JObjGetDObj(jobj)` in condition and call argument, a common matching tactic when caching a temporary would alter register allocation or instruction order.

The large `asm/melee/lb/lb_00B0.s` diff appears primarily to convert labels/comments from local zero-based labels like `.L_00000020` to absolute labels like `.L_8000B094`; the provided evidence does not show a logic rewrite there.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body/report says `lb_8000F9F8` scored `100.00%` and contributed a 156-byte match; no reviewer rationale was captured.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or clarify `JOBJ_UNK_B26` after this placeholder macro was introduced.
Search for other uses of `lb_80011C18` and `lb_80011B74` to infer the semantic meaning of bit 26.
Check whether repeated `HSD_JObjGetChild`/`HSD_JObjGetDObj` calls are required in adjacent lb_00F9 matches or can be safely cached in nonmatching cleanup.
Review subsequent `lb_00F9.h` edits for continued migration from `UNK_T`/`UNK_PARAMS` to concrete types.
Investigate why `asm/melee/lb/lb_00B0.s` relabeling appeared in this PR and whether it was a tool/config artifact rather than a source-level change.
